hint: For pm times from 1:00 pm onwards, add 12 to the hour.
explanation: Add 12 to the hour: $8 + 12 = 20$. The minutes stay the same, so $8{:}15\text{ pm} = 20{:}15$.

hint: For hours from 13 to 23, subtract 12 to get the pm time.
explanation: Subtract 12 from the hour: $13 - 12 = 1$. The minutes stay the same, so $13{:}40 = 1{:}40\text{ pm}$.

stem: A bus departs at $10{:}25$ and arrives at $13{:}10$ the same day. How many minutes does the journey take?
hint: Find the difference in hours and minutes separately, then convert to minutes.
explanation: From $10{:}25$ to $13{:}25$ is $3$ hours $= 180$ minutes. From $13{:}25$ back to $13{:}10$ is $-15$ minutes. So the journey takes $180 - 15 = 165$ minutes.

stem: A film starts at $19{:}15$ and ends at $21{:}50$. How many minutes long is the film?
hint: Calculate hours difference then minutes difference separately.
explanation: From $19{:}15$ to $21{:}15$ is $2$ hours $= 120$ minutes. From $21{:}15$ to $21{:}50$ is $35$ minutes. Total: $120 + 35 = 155$ minutes.

stem: Sydney uses AEST (UTC+10). When it is $3{:}00\text{ pm}$ (15:00) in Sydney, what is the UTC time?
hint: UTC+10 means Sydney is 10 hours ahead of UTC. Subtract 10 hours to convert to UTC.
explanation: Sydney (AEST) is UTC+10, meaning Sydney is 10 hours ahead of UTC. Subtract 10 hours: $15{:}00 - 10 = 05{:}00$ UTC $= 5{:}00\text{ am}$.

stem: Which UTC offset corresponds to AEDT (Australian Eastern Daylight Time), observed during summer in NSW and Victoria?
hint: Daylight saving adds 1 hour to the standard time offset. AEST is UTC+10.
explanation: AEST (standard time) is UTC+10. During daylight saving, clocks move forward 1 hour, making AEDT = $\text{UTC}+10+1 = \text{UTC}+11$.

stem: Adelaide uses ACST (UTC+9:30). How many minutes ahead of UTC is Adelaide?
explanation: ACST is UTC+9:30, meaning Adelaide is $9$ hours and $30$ minutes ahead of UTC. Converting: $9 \times 60 + 30 = 540 + 30 = 570$ minutes.

stem: During daylight saving, Sydney observes AEDT (UTC+11) and Perth uses AWST (UTC+8). How many hours ahead of Perth is Sydney during this period?
hint: Subtract the smaller UTC offset from the larger one.
explanation: Sydney is UTC+11 and Perth is UTC+8. The difference is $11 - 8 = 3$ hours. Sydney is $3$ hours ahead of Perth.

stem: A flight departs Sydney (AEST, UTC+10) at $9{:}00\text{ pm}$ on Monday and takes 8 hours to reach Singapore (SGT, UTC+8). At what hour does the flight arrive in Singapore? Give your answer as a whole number in 24-hour format (e.g. enter $14$ for $2{:}00\text{ pm}$ or $3$ for $3{:}00\text{ am}$).
hint: Convert the departure to UTC first, add the flight duration, then convert to Singapore time.
explanation: Departure: $21{:}00$ AEST (UTC+10) $\to$ UTC: $21 - 10 = 11{:}00$ UTC Monday. Add 8 hours: $11{:}00 + 8 = 19{:}00$ UTC Monday. Convert to Singapore (UTC+8): $19 + 8 = 27{:}00 \to 03{:}00$ Tuesday. The flight arrives at $3{:}00\text{ am}$ (03:00) in Singapore.

stem: A live concert in New York (UTC$-$5) begins at $8{:}00\text{ pm}$ on Saturday. At what hour (24-hour format, as a whole number) does the concert begin for a viewer in Melbourne observing AEDT (UTC+11)?
hint: Convert the New York time to UTC first (add 5 hours), then add 11 hours for Melbourne.
explanation: New York (UTC$-$5): $20{:}00 \to$ UTC: $20 + 5 = 01{:}00$ Sunday. Melbourne (AEDT, UTC+11): $01{:}00 + 11 = 12{:}00$ Sunday. The concert starts at $12{:}00$ noon (12) on Sunday in Melbourne.

stem: A flight departs Tokyo (JST, UTC+9) at $11{:}00\text{ am}$ on Wednesday and takes 10 hours to reach London (UTC+0). At what local London time does the flight arrive?
hint: Convert the Tokyo departure to UTC, then add the flight duration. London is UTC+0, so its time equals UTC.
explanation: Departure: $11{:}00$ JST (UTC+9) $\to$ UTC: $11 - 9 = 02{:}00$ UTC Wednesday. Add 10 hours: $02{:}00 + 10 = 12{:}00$ UTC. Since London is UTC+0, arrival is $12{:}00$ noon local time on Wednesday.

stem: A webinar is scheduled for $10{:}00\text{ am}$ UTC on Thursday. A participant in Dubai (UTC+4) wants to know their local start time. At what hour (24-hour format, as a whole number) does the webinar begin in Dubai?
hint: UTC+4 means Dubai is 4 hours ahead of UTC. Add 4 hours to the UTC time.
explanation: Dubai is UTC+4, meaning Dubai time $=$ UTC time $+ 4$ hours. $10{:}00 + 4 = 14{:}00$. The webinar begins at $14{:}00$ (2:00 pm) in Dubai.
